kernel_contract.read: This is a producer-latency surface for the future fused sparse-MLA backward kernel. It is not a promoted full sparse-attention backward implementation.

rejected_micro_optimizations.name: softmax_dot_4way_per_head_shared_reduction
rejected_micro_optimizations.read: Splitting the row term sum_d grad_output * output across four threads per head regressed because the added shared-memory traffic and synchronization outweighed any scalar dot parallelism. This rejects the simple row-term dot split; it does not reject a lower-level fused row-term design inside a persistent producer.

rejected_micro_optimizations.name: kv_lds_double_buffer
rejected_micro_optimizations.read: Ping-ponging two LDS KV pages trims the non-MFMA floor by only 0.016 ms but regresses the full score+grad producer by 0.064 ms. The source keeps the macro for reproducibility, but the retained schedule remains single-buffer because the full producer is the promotion gate.

modes.read: Route, row-term, selected metadata, LDS KV staging, and barriers; probability/scaled-dP sink and CK score/grad MFMA disabled.
Route, row-term, selected metadata, LDS staging, barriers, and probability bookkeeping with CK score/grad MFMA disabled.
Mode 0 plus QK score MFMA and score/prob writeback, with dO.V grad-prob MFMA disabled.
Full retained producer: row terms plus QK score MFMA plus dO.V grad-prob MFMA plus prob/scaled-dP bookkeeping.

producer_mode_split.read: The producer wall is split, not a single dKV write problem: route/row/KV staging/barrier floor is 2.06 ms, scalar probability bookkeeping is only 0.19 ms, QK score replay adds 1.53 ms, and dO.V grad-prob replay adds 1.02 ms. A production kernel needs persistent/cooperative score-page consumption and fewer stage barriers, not only final dKV accumulation tuning.

decision.read: Serial token chunking increases modeled coalescing opportunity but regresses realized latency because it reduces token-level parallelism and serializes score/prob/scaled-dP replay inside one CTA. The next real fix is a HIP/CK/rocWMMA cooperative or persistent producer-consumer design where a tiny score page is consumed by D-parallel dQ/dKV workers without moving through HBM, not a simple token loop in one CTA.

next_kernel_contract: Keep token-level parallelism at seq_chunk=1 unless a persistent/cooperative kernel can overlap producer and D consumers.
Add a real score-page handoff to D-parallel dQ/dKV consumers only if producer-only latency can move toward the 3.16 ms budget.
Do not run e2e TFLOPs until a source-boundary dQ+dKV kernel beats the 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward boundary.
